solution.p: We will factor 25r^{2}-9 where a=25, b=0, and c=-9.

Difference of Squares Formula: The formula for a difference of squares is a^2-b^2 = (a+b)(a-b) Note that the polynomial 25r^{2}-9 matches the formula's right side, and we have: \begin{aligned}[t]25r^{2}-9\amp =\left(5r\right)^2-\left(3\right)^2\newline \amp =\left(5r+3\right)\mathopen{}\left(5r-3\right)\end{aligned}
AC Method: In this method, we first calculate the product of a and c in ax^2+bx+c. For this problem, we have (25) \cdot (-9) = -225.

The number -225 can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways:  egin{array}{lll}1(-225)\amp -1(225)\amp 3(-75)\amp -3(75)
ewline5(-45)\amp -5(45)\amp 9(-25)\amp -9(25)
ewline15(-15)\amp -15(15)\amp \end{array} Looking at the corresponding sums, we want a sum of 0, and this sum happens with (-15)+15=0 So we write  egin{aligned}[t]25r^{2}-9\amp =25r^{2}-15r+15r-9
ewline \amp =5r\mathopen{}\left(5r-3ight)+3\mathopen{}\left(5r-3ight)
ewline \amp =\left(5r+3ight)\mathopen{}\left(5r-3ight)\end{aligned}Generic Rectangles Method: First, we set up generic rectangles by putting the first term and third term in 25r^{2}-9 into the top left and bottom right rectangles:
The first term's coefficient, 25, can be factored into the product of two numbers (where the first factor is positive) in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(25)\amp 5(5)\amp \end{array}
The third term, -9, can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(-9)\amp -1(9)\amp 3(-3)\amp -3(3)\newline\end{array}

We put each pair into the corresponding places next to those generic rectangles, and try to match the area of those rectangles with 25r^{2}-9 by guess-and-check. The following generic rectangles show the solution:
Dimensions of those generic rectangles in the diagram give us the solution: \begin{aligned}[t]25r^{2}-9\amp =\left(5r+3\right)\mathopen{}\left(5r-3\right)\end{aligned}
